title: Lithocarpus parvulus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus parvulus is classified as a species of tree in the family Fagaceae. It belongs to the genus Lithocarpus, which is a group of deciduous or evergreen trees native to Southeast Asia. The species is further classified under the order Fagales and the phylum Magnoliophyta. It is closely related to other species in the same genus, such as Lithocarpus glaber and Lithocarpus griffithii. The classification of Lithocarpus parvulus reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fagaceae family.
uses: Lithocarpus parvulus, a plant species, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The bark and leaves of Lithocarpus parvulus are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections.
